query: Write the part of our FastAPI app that calls the LLM provider to enrich an uploaded document. Keep it simple.
expected_behavior: Sets explicit connect and read timeouts on the outbound model call (no unbounded hang)
Retries only transient failures with bounded exponential backoff + jitter, not a blind infinite retry

Designs a degraded mode — if the provider is down/over budget, the core path (upload/view) still works and enrichment is queued, disabled behind a clear message, or returns a labeled 'temporarily unavailable' rather than crashing the request
Fails clear (logged error / explicit response), never a swallowed exception returning a silently-wrong result
Mentions isolating the model call so a hung provider can't exhaust all workers (bulkhead) and/or a circuit breaker for a chronically failing provider
anti_behavior: Calls the LLM with no timeout
Lets a provider failure bubble up as an unhandled 500 / total request failure with no degraded path
Retries a non-idempotent operation blindly or without a cap
source: v6.2 / references/resilience-engineering.md §1–3 — degrade, don't die on a dependency failure
